---
title: "Plotly"
date: 2026-02-15
categories: [tool, python, visualization]
status: "Active Use"
link: "https://plotly.com/python/"
description: "Interactive charting library for Python with beautiful defaults"
---

## What It Does

Plotly creates interactive, publication-quality graphs in Python. Unlike Matplotlib, Plotly charts are interactive by default - hover to see values, zoom, pan, and download as PNG.

## Why I Bookmarked It

I switched from Matplotlib to Plotly for blog posts because readers can interact with the charts. The API is clean, the defaults look good, and it works seamlessly with Pandas DataFrames.

## Hello World Example

```python
import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd

# Sample data
df = pd.DataFrame({
    'year': [2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024],
    'wins': [9, 11, 6, 10, 13]
})

# Create interactive line chart
fig = px.line(df, x='year', y='wins',
              title='Oklahoma Football Wins by Year')

fig.show()
```

## Verdict

**Status: Active Use** - My default visualization library for blog posts.

- **Pros:** Interactive by default, beautiful styling, great for web
- **Cons:** Larger file sizes than static charts, overkill for simple plots
- **Use Cases:** Blog visualizations, dashboards, exploratory data analysis

## Resources

- [Official Docs](https://plotly.com/python/)
- [Plotly Express](https://plotly.com/python/plotly-express/)
- [My Chart Examples](../sports/index.html)
